verbatim_text: And just to define these real quick. So failures of coherence refers to the ability or inability for AI systems to generate outputs that are compatible with the current state of the world the user or themselves. So one example is hallucinations that comes from the AI system not generating things that are coherent, factually coherent with the current state of the world. So updatability refers to how an AI system can update in the presence of new information about the world the user or themselves. So, for example, continual learning requires the ability for AI system to update to account for the new factual changes about the world. OK, so also typically the AI systems outputs inform the next inputs it receives. So I'm going to also draw an arrow between the output and the input like this. I'm going to move this diagram up here and then we all can keep chaining together these state updates to get something like this. And also to make this diagram a little bit more concrete. I'm going to talk about exactly what it looks like for the world the user and the self model with some concrete examples. So one example for world models is language generation. So suppose we have Janet bought the book. This should induce the AI system to represent the world state as one where Janet now possesses a book. Next the AI should generate something coherent to this world model like Janet gave Jack the book.
